title: Conditional
caption: The letter arrived on a Thursday, hand-delivered by a city courier on a bicycle who barely paused before pedaling away. Mr. Chen stood at the garden gate with the official envelope in his weathered hands, and Chloe watched his face as he read it aloud — the words careful and measured, each one carrying weight. The news was good and not quite enough: the garden qualified for heritage protection under the Community Gardens Preservation Act, but a final public hearing stood between them and certainty. 'Conditional,' Chloe said, her eyes bright. 'That's good, right?' Mr. Chen folded the letter with precision. 'It means we're not done yet.' By Friday the corner table at Marco's had become a war council, the usual crowd augmented by every neighbor who had a stake in those soil beds. Chloe stood at the head of the table, the letter in one hand, her other hand tracing the timeline on a napkin. Father Bernard listened with his hands folded. Detective Park puffed out his chest and declared he would attend 'with my notes.' Rosie bounced in her seat. 'Everyone has to come!' Marco emerged from the kitchen with a tray of fresh rolls, steam rising, and Luna the black cat settled onto the back of an empty chair as if she'd been there all along. By Friday, they had a plan.
location: Willowmere Community Garden and Marco's Bakery
story_notes: The heritage decision arrives: the garden qualifies, but a final public hearing is required before it's confirmed.
